title: Medium is the Massage — Analysis Database (v1.2)
description: Structured analysis of each spread in the original book. Each entry represents one PDF page, which typically contains a two-page spread (left + right book pages). Front matter and back matter may be single pages. v1.2 adds theoretically grounded controlled vocabularies (Kress & van Leeuwen, Barthes, Drucker), structured image-text relation fields, confidence indicators, and per-entry analyst metadata.

metadata.description: Information about the source material and analysis run.

schema_theoretical_basis.description: Theoretical frameworks operationalized by this schema.

spreads.description: One entry per PDF page, in order.

id.description: Unique identifier. Format: 'spread_NNN' using zero-padded PDF page number.

book_pages.description: The original book page number(s) this PDF page covers. A spread contains two book pages (e.g., [62, 63]). Front/back matter may be a single page ([1]) or unnumbered ([]).

analyst.description: Identifier for the analyst (human name, model ID, or 'multi' for collaborative).

analysis_method.description: The human-AI collaboration mode used for this entry.

spread_type.description: The dominant compositional mode of this spread. Classification rules: 'image_dominant' = image occupies >=70% of spread area AND text is subordinate (captions, small overlaid text only). 'text_with_specific_image' = substantial text paragraph + image that makes a specific claim related to the text content. 'text_with_mood_image' = substantial text + image that establishes tone without specific referential content. 'typography_as_design' = text IS the visual content (no separate images; typographic form carries the argument). 'collage' = 3+ distinct visual elements from different sources combined on a single spread. 'symbol_or_graphic' = abstract or symbolic visual element dominates (no photographic content). 'quote_only' = attributed quotation with no body text by the authors. 'press_photo_with_title' = press photograph paired with a title or headline element. 'text_only' = body text with no images or display typography. 'rotated_page' = page requires physical rotation to read. 'credits_or_colophon' = credits, copyright, or colophon page. 'title_page' = title page of the book. 'other' = does not fit any of the above categories.

orientation.description: Physical orientation. Most body pages are landscape spreads. Some pages require the reader to rotate the book.

text.description: All text content on this spread.

body_text.description: McLuhan's/Fiore's own prose. Transcribed verbatim. Empty string if no body text.

display_text.description: Text used as a typographic design element (e.g., giant 'and how!', section titles like 'EDUCATION'). May overlap with body_text if a phrase serves both functions.

captions.description: Image captions, photo credits visible on the page, or other small contextual text.

page_numbers_visible.description: Page numbers actually printed on this spread, if visible.

quotations.description: Every quotation from a named author on this spread.

author.description: Name as attributed on the page.

author_context.description: Brief identification: who this person is/was and why they're relevant here.

source_work.description: If identifiable, the work the quote is drawn from.

relationship_to_argument.description: How the quote functions on this spread: does it set up the argument, provide authority, offer counterpoint, serve as epigraph, etc.?

visual_treatment.description: How the quote is typographically presented: small italic, large display, overlaid on image, etc.

images.description: Every distinct image on this spread. Includes photographs, illustrations, graphic symbols, and reproductions. Does NOT include typography-as-design (that goes in text.display_text).

subject.description: What the image depicts. Be specific: identifiable people, events, objects, scenes.

source_credit.description: Photographer/source as listed in the credits page (p.84 of PDF). Cross-reference with page number.

estimated_date.description: Approximate date or era of the image, if determinable.

composition.description: Brief description of composition: framing, angle, contrast, key visual qualities.

scale.description: How much of the page area the image occupies.

relationship_to_text.description: How this image relates to the text on the spread, classified using Barthes' image-text relations and Martinec & Salway's extensions.

primary_relation.description: The dominant relationship between this image and the spread's text.

barthes_mode.description: Barthes' classification: does the text fix the image's meaning (anchorage), or do text and image contribute complementary information (relay)?

description.description: Prose elaboration of the relationship. Required when primary_relation is 'contradicts', 'ironizes', 'literalizes', or 'serves_as_metaphor'.

interactive_meaning.description: Kress & van Leeuwen's interactive metafunction: the relationship between depicted participant and viewer.

contact.description: Does the depicted participant gaze at the viewer (demand) or not (offer)? Not applicable for abstract/symbolic images.

social_distance.description: Implied distance: intimate (face/head only), close social (head and shoulders), far social (full figure), impersonal (many figures or wide view).

attitude_angle.description: Viewer's implied orientation: frontal (involvement), oblique (detachment), overhead (viewer power), low angle (depicted power).

design.description: The visual design of the spread as a whole.

layout_description.description: Prose description of the spatial arrangement: where text and images sit, how space is used, the relationship between left and right pages.

body_font_style.description: General description of body text typography: serif/sans, approximate size, weight, column width, justification.

display_font_style.description: Typography of display/headline text if present: font style, approximate size relative to page, weight.

special_treatments.description: Any unusual typographic choices: reversed text, rotated text, text overlaid on image, text as texture, letterspacing effects, etc.

color_and_tone.description: The tonal quality of the spread. Since this is a black-and-white book, captures contrast level, dominant tone, and any notable tonal treatments.

contrast.description: Degree of tonal contrast between darkest and lightest areas.

dominant_tone.description: Whether the spread reads as predominantly dark, predominantly light, or balanced.

description.description: Optional prose elaboration for unusual tonal treatments.

white_space.description: How much empty/breathing space exists on the spread. Percentage guidelines: abundant >= 40% of spread area is empty, moderate = 15-40%, minimal = 5-15%, none < 5%. These are visual estimates, not measurements.

visual_density.description: Overall information density of the spread. Percentage guidelines: sparse = few elements with generous spacing, moderate = balanced content-to-space ratio, dense = most of the spread occupied by content with little breathing room, overwhelming = every area filled with competing visual elements demanding simultaneous attention.

left_right_relationship.description: How the two pages of the spread relate to each other: mirror, contrast, continuation, call-and-response, independent, one dominates, etc.

information_value.description: Kress & van Leeuwen's information value: the meaning carried by spatial placement on the spread.

left_right.description: Does the spread follow the conventional Given (left) / New (right) structure, reverse it, balance both sides equally, or operate as effectively a single page?

top_bottom.description: Does the spread follow the Ideal (top) / Real (bottom) structure?

center_margin.description: Is there a dominant center with subordinate margins, or is information distributed?

compositional_framing.description: Kress & van Leeuwen's framing: how strongly elements on the spread are separated from one another. 'Strongly framed' = clear boundaries between elements (frames, white space, color breaks). 'Weakly framed' = elements flow into one another (overlaps, shared backgrounds, cross-gutter continuity). 'Mixed' = some elements framed, others flowing.

rhetoric.description: The rhetorical layer — what the spread DOES, not just what it contains. This is the most important section.

argument.description: The core argument or claim being made on this spread, stated plainly. What is McLuhan saying here?

rhetorical_strategy.description: How the argument is being made, classified using a controlled 15-term vocabulary.

primary.description: The dominant rhetorical strategy on this spread.

secondary.description: Up to two secondary strategies operating simultaneously.

strategy_description.description: Prose elaboration of how the rhetorical strategy operates on this spread. Required when primary strategy is 'confrontation', 'demonstration', 'interpellation', or 'defamiliarization'. Should name specific formal mechanisms.

design_enacts_argument.description: Does the visual design itself perform or embody the argument (e.g., a spread about speed that feels fast, a spread about fragmentation that is fragmented)?

design_argument_description.description: If design_enacts_argument is true, explain how. This is the most valuable analytical insight.

reader_experience.description: What is the intended effect on the reader? Disorientation, recognition, amusement, unease, exhilaration, confrontation, contemplation, etc.

mcluhan_concepts.description: Which McLuhan concepts are active on this spread. Select from the enumerated list.

multi_spread_patterns.description: If this spread participates in a rhetorical pattern spanning multiple spreads (e.g., progressive density increase, dark/light alternation, thematic accumulation across 3+ spreads), describe the pattern and reference the spread IDs involved. Null if the spread operates independently.

confidence.description: Analyst's self-reported confidence in this section's analysis. 'high' = unambiguous, any competent analyst would agree. 'moderate' = reasonable alternatives exist. 'low' = best guess, significant uncertainty.

themes.description: Thematic tagging for mapping to contemporary domains.

original_themes.description: Themes as they appear in the original. Free-text tags, but use consistent vocabulary across spreads. Examples: 'privacy', 'education', 'the child', 'speed', 'environment', 'technology_as_extension', 'authorship', 'electric_age', 'participation', 'obsolescence', etc.

contemporary_domain_candidates.description: Which of the pre-defined contemporary thematic domains this spread maps to. A spread may map to multiple domains.

movement_mapping.description: Which movement(s) in the contemporary book's three-movement structure this spread most naturally maps to. Based on the Theoretical Framework's Prologue + three-Movement architecture (Environment / Acceleration / Dreamscape). Most spreads will map to a single movement; some may bridge two (especially near structural transitions). This field works alongside contemporary_domain_candidates — domains indicate WHAT themes are active, movement_mapping indicates WHERE in the new book's arc they belong.

mapping_rationale.description: Brief explanation of why this spread maps to the indicated contemporary domains. What is the conceptual bridge?

confidence.description: Analyst's self-reported confidence in this section's analysis. 'high' = unambiguous, any competent analyst would agree. 'moderate' = reasonable alternatives exist. 'low' = best guess, significant uncertainty.

progression.description: How this spread relates to the sequence of the book.

pace_shift.description: Does this spread speed up, slow down, maintain, or break the rhythm relative to the immediately preceding spread (spread N-1)? Consider: amount of text, number of visual elements, visual density, and demand on reader attention.

thematic_function.description: What role this spread plays in the thematic arc.

relationship_to_previous.description: How this spread connects to the one before it: continuation, contrast, escalation, pivot, non sequitur, etc.

relationship_to_next.description: How this spread sets up the one after it.

confidence.description: Analyst's self-reported confidence in this section's analysis. 'high' = unambiguous, any competent analyst would agree. 'moderate' = reasonable alternatives exist. 'low' = best guess, significant uncertainty.

notes.description: Any additional observations that don't fit the above categories. OCR uncertainties, damaged/illegible areas, interesting details, cross-references to other spreads.

thematic_arc.description: A summary analysis of the book's overall thematic structure, produced after all spreads are analyzed. Used to identify the original's implicit sections and map them to the contemporary book's Prologue + three-Movement architecture.

sections.description: The book's implicit sections/movements (it has no formal chapters). Each section groups consecutive spreads that share a thematic focus.

title.description: A descriptive title for this section.

summary.description: What this section accomplishes in the book's argument.

movement_correspondence.description: Which movement in the contemporary book's structure (prologue, movement_1_environment, movement_2_acceleration, hinge_m2_m3, movement_3_dreamscape) this section most closely corresponds to.

overall_arc.description: A narrative description of how the book moves from beginning to end: how themes are introduced, layered, and (non-)resolved.

rhythm_pattern.description: The visual/pacing rhythm of the book: where it's dense vs. sparse, fast vs. slow, loud vs. quiet.

appearances.description: Spread IDs where this motif appears.

function.description: What this recurring element does across the book.
